title: Sentinel Shield operational event
description: ONE line of an opt-in JSONL operational-event stream produced by scripts/lib/operational-events.sh (oe_emit). Each line is an independent, self-describing object recording one phase-transition of a Sentinel Shield operation (acquisition, install, sync, migration, bootstrap, doctor, pipeline, recovery, security scanning, evidence collection, artifact verification, release finalization, health). A stream is grouped by correlation_id (shared across an operation AND the recovery it triggers) and split by operation_id (unique per operation), so a failed operation and its recovery can be reconstructed in order. The object carries NO secrets, NO raw tokens, NO email, and NO repo-local absolute path: the target identity is a NON-REVERSIBLE 12-hex sha256 prefix and the free-text next_action is passed through the unified redaction library. The closed enums below are kept in lockstep with the oe_* vocabulary accessors; tests/prod/254-operational-health.sh cross-checks the two so the vocabulary can never drift from the code.

schema.description: Fixed discriminator for this object shape.

schema_version.description: Event-model schema version. Fixed at '1' for this contract.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the event was emitted.

command.description: The high-level operation that emitted the event.

event_type.description: The kind of transition this event records.

reason_code.description: A STABLE, machine-consumable reason code (snake/kebab). Carries no free text and no secret.

status.description: Outcome status at this point in the operation.

component.description: The subsystem that emitted the event (e.g. transaction, scanner, source-verification).

retryability.description: Whether the recorded condition is safe to retry automatically, requires manual intervention, or is not applicable.

correlation_id.description: Shared across an operation AND any operation it triggers (e.g. a failed install and its recovery), so a full incident can be reconstructed.

operation_id.description: Unique to a single operation. Distinct operations under one correlation_id carry distinct operation_ids.

target.description: NON-REVERSIBLE target identity: 'target:<12-hex>' (sha256 prefix of the absolute target path), or 'target:none'/'target:unknown'. NEVER a raw path.

elapsed_ms.description: Best-effort (monotonic-where-available) elapsed milliseconds since the operation's start clock, or null when not measured.

next_action.description: Optional operator hint for what to do next. Passed through the unified redaction library; carries no secret or absolute path.
